description:
A log file from the production web-application has been copied into the
    training container so that you can practise building automated 5xx error
    alerts.

    1.  The log file is located at  
        /home/user/logs/webapp-2023-09-16.log.gz  
        and is a standard GNU-zip archive that expands to a single, plain-text
        file in CLF-like format:

              2023-09-16T14:23:01Z GET /some/endpoint 200 OK 123ms

        i.e.  
        • ISO-8601 timestamp (UTC, no spaces)  
        • HTTP verb  
        • URI path (no query string)  
        • numeric status code  
        • textual reason phrase (underscores instead of spaces)  
        • latency with trailing “ms”

        The file already contains a mix of 2xx, 3xx, 4xx and 5xx responses.

    2.  Your goal is to automate a 5xx monitoring routine for
        2023-09-16 *only*:

        a.  Decompress the file into the same /home/user/logs directory.
        b.  Filter every line whose status code is in the inclusive range
            500-599.  
            • Ignore any other status codes.  
            • Retain the original lines for counting; you do not need to edit
              them.
        c.  Tally the number of 5xx lines *per unique URI path*.
        d.  Produce a summary CSV and (if required) an alert file, following
            the exact specifications below.

    3.  File-system requirements you must meet:

        3.1  Directory  
             /home/user/alerts  
             If it does not exist, create it with default user permissions.

        3.2  Summary CSV  
             Path              : /home/user/alerts/sep16_500_summary.csv  
             Required format   :
                 • First line (header)     : Endpoint,Count
                 • One subsequent line per unique URI path.
                 • Each line exactly:

                       <uri-path>,<integer-count>

                 • Sort lines in *descending* order by Count, and use
                   ASCIIbetical order of the URI path to break ties.
                 • The file **must** end with a single trailing newline
                   character (Unix LF).

        3.3  Alert file  
             A single-line text file must be created *if and only if* the
             total number of 5xx responses found for 2023-09-16 is **strictly
             greater than 7**.  
             Path            : /home/user/alerts/ALERT_SEP16.txt  
             Exact content   :

                 ALERT 2023-09-16: <TOTAL> server errors detected (threshold: 7)

             Replace <TOTAL> with the integer count.  The file must also
             terminate with exactly one Unix LF.

    4.  Verification hints (do **not** reveal real numbers here):
        • The test harness will gunzip the archive, run your commands and
          check sep16_500_summary.csv for the correct header, ordering, counts
          and trailing newline.  
        • If the total 5xx count exceeds 7, the harness will also check for
          the alert file and validate that the single line matches the
          template precisely.

    5.  Think sequentially.  A realistic workflow might be:
        • Decompress → grep/awk to isolate 5xx → sort/uniq/awk to count →
          output CSV → if-statement on total → create alert file when needed.

    Deliverables that *must* exist after you finish:
        • /home/user/logs/webapp-2023-09-16.log         (decompressed file)
        • /home/user/alerts/sep16_500_summary.csv       (summary report)
        • /home/user/alerts/ALERT_SEP16.txt             (only if threshold met)

    The evaluation script will fail if any file is missing, mis-named, placed
    in a different directory, has incorrect line endings, wrong sort order,
    or deviates from the specified text exactly.  Good luck!
truth:
<!--  Pre-existing artefact  -->
    <provided>
        <file path="/home/user/logs/webapp-2023-09-16.log.gz" type="gzip">
            <![CDATA[
2023-09-16T00:01:02Z GET /api/users 200 OK 110ms
2023-09-16T00:02:03Z POST /api/users 201 Created 95ms
2023-09-16T00:02:55Z GET /api/products 503 Service_Unavailable 220ms
2023-09-16T00:03:21Z GET /api/orders 200 OK 180ms
2023-09-16T00:04:12Z GET /api/products 500 Internal_Server_Error 210ms
2023-09-16T00:05:37Z GET /api/users 504 Gateway_Timeout 330ms
2023-09-16T00:06:30Z GET /api/products 502 Bad_Gateway 250ms
2023-09-16T00:07:11Z GET /api/users 200 OK 120ms
2023-09-16T00:07:30Z POST /api/login 500 Internal_Server_Error 150ms
2023-09-16T00:08:04Z POST /api/users/logout 200 OK 90ms
2023-09-16T00:09:46Z GET /api/products 503 Service_Unavailable 225ms
2023-09-16T00:10:22Z GET /api/users 200 OK 115ms
2023-09-16T00:11:17Z GET /api/products 200 OK 185ms
2023-09-16T00:11:44Z POST /api/orders 500 Internal_Server_Error 310ms
2023-09-16T00:12:10Z GET /api/users 502 Bad_Gateway 280ms
            ]]>
        </file>
    </provided>

    <!--  Expected artefacts AFTER the agent completes the task  -->
    <expected>
        <file path="/home/user/logs/webapp-2023-09-16.log" type="text">
            <!-- same 15 lines as above, uncompressed -->
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/alerts/sep16_500_summary.csv" type="text">
            <![CDATA[
Endpoint,Count
/api/products,4
/api/users,2
/api/login,1
/api/orders,1
]]>
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/alerts/ALERT_SEP16.txt" type="text">
            <![CDATA[
ALERT 2023-09-16: 8 server errors detected (threshold: 7)
]]>
        </file>
    </expected>
